[ HERO MEDIA — TIER 1 ] Cinematic video loop preferred. 4K still as fallback. Best footage: slow-pan shop floor, hands inspecting components, wire harness builds in progress, loading dock activity, packaging lines, sortation in motion, drone exterior of facility.

Avoid: staged handshakes, smiling stock-photo executives, fake networking, generic boardrooms.

Specs: 1920×1080 video, 15–25 sec loop, no audio, muted color grade. Or 3840×2160 still. Dark left-side gradient is auto-applied for text legibility.

Manufacturing Partnerships

The strongest manufacturing companies rarely grow alone.

Manufacturing performance is shaped by the strength of the ecosystem surrounding it — suppliers, operational partners, workforce infrastructure, technical resources, and industry relationships working together effectively.

RELY was built to strengthen those connections.

— The Ecosystem in Practice
Supplier Network
Customer Partnerships

Industry Associations

Technical & Training Partners

Strong manufacturing ecosystems create stronger manufacturers.

[ FIGURE 03.A — SUPPLIER OPERATIONS ] Single hero image, vertical or square.

Best shots: • Close-up of components arriving on pallets at receiving dock
• Operator inspecting incoming supplier parts
• Tight detail of raw stock or sub-components staged for production
• Side-by-side: supplier handoff to RELY team

Tone: Industrial, cinematic, natural lighting. Mid-action, not posed.

Specs: 1080×1350 minimum, vertical 4:5 ideal. Will fill left half of section.

Why It Matters

Why RELY Invests Heavily in Supplier Relationships.

Supply chain performance remains one of the most underleveraged competitive advantages in manufacturing.

RELY prioritizes long-term supplier relationships because stronger, more capable, and more stable supply partners directly improve what we can deliver to customers.

  • Consistency and responsiveness
  • Long-term operational alignment
  • Shared growth opportunities
  • Quality and execution standards
  • Open communication and problem-solving
"
We do not simply purchase from suppliers. We build long-term operational partnerships with them.
Operational Philosophy

Building Relationships Beyond Purchase Orders.

Strong manufacturing networks are built through active collaboration, not isolated transactions.

01
Strong companies identified
02
Relationships developed
03
Opportunities shared
04
Network strengthened
05
Customers scale faster

The best manufacturing ecosystems are not closed systems.

They are networks where capable companies help strengthen one another over time.

— Tile 01 / Automotive Tier 1 wire harness or sub-assembly on a workbench. Tight on hands and components.
— Tile 02 / Industrial Control panel build or electrical assembly. Wide enough to show the work, tight enough to feel premium.
— Tile 03 / Wire Harness Detail shot — connectors, braiding, or labeled harness on a build board.
— Tile 04 / Consumer Kitting or packaging line for a DTC brand. Color and product variety welcome.
— Tile 05 / Fulfillment Shipping cartons stacked, labeled, ready to load. Or a dock door view.
The Customer Side

Operating as an Extension of Customer Brands.

RELY operates as a direct extension of customer brands and manufacturing operations. That responsibility shapes how we approach execution, responsiveness, quality, and operational support across every engagement.

Most customer relationships begin with a focused operational need. Over time, many evolve into multi-year partnerships built on execution consistency, trust, and shared growth.

  • — I. Fortune 500 brands with heavy SKU mix and need for customization
  • — II. OEMs and Tier 1/2 suppliers with potential line-down situations
  • — III. High-growth consumer brands scaling through TikTok Shop and e-commerce
  • — IV. Manufacturers who are running out of space
  • — V. Lights-out molders looking to expand what they bid on
  • — VI. Machine shops tired of using their machinists for value-added work
What We Absorb

The execution-heavy work that slows internal teams down.

RELY helps manufacturers protect internal focus while maintaining operational flexibility.

The Broader Ecosystem

Industry & Community Partnerships.

Strengthening manufacturing requires active participation in the broader ecosystem surrounding it. RELY does not view industry groups, workforce organizations, and leadership networks as passive affiliations. We treat them as critical infrastructure for long-term manufacturing growth.

— Pillar I.

Education & Workforce Development

  • Milwaukee Area Technical College
  • Waukesha County Technical College
  • University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee
— Pillar II.

Industry Organizations

  • IPC / WHMA
  • MICHauto
  • MEMA
  • Food and Beverage Wisconsin
  • Women in Manufacturing
— Pillar III.

Leadership Networks

  • Waukesha County Business Alliance Manufacturing Executive Council
  • Metropolitan Milwaukee Association of Commerce (MMAC)
  • Vistage
  • Executive Agenda
  • Paranet
Active Participation, Not Affiliation

RELY does not just list these organizations. We show up — at events, summits, technical training, and policy tables — to strengthen the ecosystem we operate in.

— IPC/WHMA Wire Processing Expo
— Pillar II / Industry Organizations

Where the work gets sharper.

Jim Lowe, VP of Engineering and Technology, and Clay Giese, National Sales Manager, at the IPC/WHMA Wire Processing Expo. RELY exhibits and attends technical training at industry events to stay current on emerging methods, materials, and standards.

— Wisconsin Economic Summit
— Pillar I / Workforce Development

Building workforce infrastructure.

Katie Malnight Meisinger and Plant Manager Kimmi Roehrborn with the ThinkAbility Wisconsin cohort, Islands of Brilliance, and DSAW alongside Governor Tony Evers at the Wisconsin Economic Summit.

What This Means for You

The difference a network makes.

Working with RELY means gaining access to more than production support. It means gaining access to a manufacturing network built around operational execution, trusted relationships, workforce development, supplier connectivity, and long-term scalability.

Without the Right Network

The Default State.

  • Limited supplier visibility
  • More operational friction
  • Slower scaling
  • Isolated decision-making
With RELY

A connected ecosystem.

  • Trusted operational relationships
  • Stronger manufacturing connectivity
  • Better scalability support
  • Broader ecosystem access

RELY strengthens the ecosystem surrounding its customers so they can scale with less friction.
